functional_requirements.title: Breathing Space Compliance
functional_requirements.description: The system shall detect when a customer enters Breathing Space (Debt Respite Scheme 2021) and automatically: freeze all credit-related alerts, suppress pre-approved offers, stop proactive communications about credit, and freeze any interest/charges on Lloyds products surfaced through the Credit Coach.

citation.source_reference: Alerts must not pressure customers in financial difficulty; must signpost to debt advice
citation.source_location: Section 8.2: FCA CONC 7 — Arrears and default

metadata.reasoning: Derived from CONC 7 compliance requirement in the product vision, grounded in domain KB PD7 Breathing Space (Debt Respite Scheme 2021). During breathing space, lenders MUST freeze interest/charges, stop enforcement, and stop contacting the customer about debt. This is a legal obligation not explicitly called out in the vision but required by the cited regulation.

metadata.reasoning: The product vision mentions right to erasure (Art. 17) but omits the corresponding right to access (Art. 15 / DSAR). Domain KB PD14 confirms DSARs must be responded to within 1 calendar month. This is a mandatory GDPR obligation for any system holding personal data.

non_functional_requirements.reasoning: Explicit security requirement. Aligned with enterprise architecture KB confirming TLS 1.3.

non_functional_requirements.title: Data Classification
non_functional_requirements.description: CRA data shall be classified as Highly Confidential and handled according to Lloyds' data classification policies.

citation.source_reference: Data classified as Highly Confidential

non_functional_requirements.reasoning: Explicit data classification requirement.

non_functional_requirements.title: Score History Retention (6 Years)
non_functional_requirements.description: Score history shall be retained for 6 years in compliance with FCA record-keeping requirements.

citation.source_reference: Score history retained for 6 years (FCA record-keeping)

non_functional_requirements.reasoning: Regulatory data retention requirement aligned with FCA SYSC 9.1.1R.

non_functional_requirements.title: Consent Records Retention
non_functional_requirements.description: Consent records shall be retained indefinitely (no expiry) to demonstrate ongoing compliance with data protection requirements. Must be retrievable within 24 hours for regulatory audit.

citation.source_reference: Consent records retained indefinitely

non_functional_requirements.reasoning: Explicit retention requirement for demonstrating GDPR compliance.

non_functional_requirements.title: WCAG 2.1 AA Compliance
non_functional_requirements.description: All Credit Coach UI components shall be WCAG 2.1 AA compliant.

citation.source_reference: WCAG 2.1 AA compliant

non_functional_requirements.reasoning: Accessibility standard. Aligned with enterprise architecture KB requirement.

non_functional_requirements.title: Screen Reader Compatibility
non_functional_requirements.description: All Credit Coach UI components shall be compatible with VoiceOver (iOS) and TalkBack (Android) screen readers, with 100% of interactive elements having accessible labels.

citation.source_reference: Screen reader compatible

non_functional_requirements.reasoning: Explicit accessibility requirement for visually impaired users.

non_functional_requirements.title: Colour-Blind Safe Visualisations
non_functional_requirements.description: Score visualisations shall use colour-blind safe palettes (tested against deuteranopia, protanopia, tritanopia) and not rely solely on colour to convey information. All charts must pass WCAG 2.1 AA contrast ratio (4.5:1 for text, 3:1 for UI components).

citation.source_reference: Colour-blind safe score visualisations

non_functional_requirements.reasoning: Explicit accessibility requirement for colour-blind users.

non_functional_requirements.title: FCA Consumer Duty Compliance
non_functional_requirements.description: The system shall comply with FCA Consumer Duty (PS22/9) including all four outcomes: products and services, price and value, consumer understanding, and consumer support.

citation.source_reference: A firm must act to deliver good outcomes for retail customers

non_functional_requirements.reasoning: Overarching regulatory requirement. Each cross-cutting rule has specific implications documented.

non_functional_requirements.title: FCA CONC 3 Financial Promotions Compliance
non_functional_requirements.description: All pre-approved offers constituting financial promotions shall comply with FCA CONC 3 requirements for clarity, fairness, and non-misleading content.

citation.source_reference: Pre-approved offers are financial promotions and must be clear, fair, and not misleading

non_functional_requirements.reasoning: Separate regulation requiring compliance. Domain KB confirms detailed requirements.

non_functional_requirements.title: FCA CONC 5 Creditworthiness Compliance
non_functional_requirements.description: Pre-approved offers shall comply with FCA CONC 5 creditworthiness assessment requirements covering credit risk and affordability.

citation.source_reference: Pre-approved offers must be based on adequate creditworthiness assessment covering both credit risk and affordability

non_functional_requirements.reasoning: Separate regulation. Source notes this generates the most FCA enforcement action.

non_functional_requirements.title: FCA CONC 7 Arrears and Default Compliance
non_functional_requirements.description: The system shall comply with FCA CONC 7 requirements regarding treatment of customers in financial difficulty, including signposting to free debt advice.

citation.source_reference: Alerts must not pressure customers in financial difficulty; must signpost to free debt advice

non_functional_requirements.reasoning: Separate regulation with specific implications for alert behaviour.

non_functional_requirements.title: UK GDPR and DPA 2018 Compliance
non_functional_requirements.description: The system shall comply with UK GDPR and Data Protection Act 2018 requirements including lawful basis, purpose limitation, data minimisation, right to erasure, DPIA, and automated decision-making provisions.

citation.source_reference: Explicit consent for CRA data retrieval; purpose limitation; data minimisation; right to erasure; DPIA required; automated decision-making provisions

non_functional_requirements.reasoning: Comprehensive data protection regulation with multiple specific requirements documented.

non_functional_requirements.title: Consumer Credit Act 1974 Compliance
non_functional_requirements.description: Pre-approved offers for regulated credit agreements shall comply with CCA requirements including pre-contract information (SECCI), cooling-off period, and unfair relationships provisions.

citation.source_reference: Pre-contract information, cooling-off period, unfair relationships

non_functional_requirements.reasoning: Foundational UK lending legislation. Non-compliance makes agreements unenforceable.

non_functional_requirements.title: AI/ML Explainability (FS2/23)
non_functional_requirements.description: Score factors and simulation logic shall be explainable to customers in plain language (Flesch reading ease score >= 60). Black-box models are not acceptable for customer-facing credit decisions. Every recommendation must include a 'why' explanation.

citation.source_reference: Score factors and simulation logic must be explainable to customers in plain language. 'Black box' models are not acceptable for customer-facing credit decisions.
citation.source_location: Section 8.5 BoE/PRA/FCA Joint Feedback on AI/ML (Explainability)

non_functional_requirements.reasoning: Regulatory expectation from FS2/23 joint feedback statement.

non_functional_requirements.title: AI Fairness and Bias Audits
non_functional_requirements.description: Improvement plans and offers shall not discriminate on protected characteristics (Equality Act 2010). Bias audits shall be conducted quarterly across demographic groups, with disparate impact ratio maintained above 0.8 (four-fifths rule).

citation.source_reference: Improvement plans and offers must not discriminate on protected characteristics; regular bias audits required across demographic groups
citation.source_location: Section 8.5 BoE/PRA/FCA Joint Feedback on AI/ML (Fairness & bias)

non_functional_requirements.reasoning: Regulatory expectation from FS2/23 and Equality Act 2010 requirement.

non_functional_requirements.title: Model Risk Management (PRA SS1/23)
non_functional_requirements.description: Credit simulation models and offer-matching algorithms shall be inventoried, risk-classified, independently validated before deployment, and revalidated at least annually. Model performance drift exceeding 5% from baseline triggers mandatory review.

citation.source_reference: Credit simulation models and offer-matching algorithms must be inventoried and risk-classified; independent validation before deployment; periodic revalidation
citation.source_location: Section 8.6 PRA SS1/23 — Model Risk Management

non_functional_requirements.reasoning: PRA supervisory statement in force since May 2024. Applies to simulation and offer-matching models.

non_functional_requirements.title: Equality Act 2010 Non-Discrimination
non_functional_requirements.description: The Credit Coach shall not directly or indirectly discriminate in service provision based on protected characteristics (age, disability, gender reassignment, marriage/civil partnership, pregnancy/maternity, race, religion/belief, sex, sexual orientation).

citation.source_reference: The Credit Coach must not directly or indirectly discriminate in the provision of services based on protected characteristics

non_functional_requirements.reasoning: Separate legislation with specific implications for action plans, offers, and simulation outputs.

non_functional_requirements.title: DPIA Completion Before Launch
non_functional_requirements.description: A Data Protection Impact Assessment shall be completed before the Credit Coach launches, as large-scale profiling of financial data triggers the DPIA requirement.

citation.source_reference: Mandatory Data Protection Impact Assessment before launch — large-scale profiling of financial data triggers DPIA requirement

non_functional_requirements.reasoning: Legal requirement under UK GDPR Art. 35. Listed as a dependency.

non_functional_requirements.title: CRA Data Quality Validation
non_functional_requirements.description: CRA data inputs shall be validated for quality and currency. Data older than 30 days shall trigger a re-fetch. Stale or incorrect data shall not drive recommendations or offers. Data quality score must be >= 95% before processing.

citation.source_reference: CRA data inputs must be validated; stale or incorrect data must not drive recommendations
citation.source_location: Section 8.5 BoE/PRA/FCA Joint Feedback on AI/ML (Data quality)

non_functional_requirements.reasoning: Regulatory expectation from FS2/23 for data quality in AI systems.

non_functional_requirements.title: DUAA 2025 Automated Decision-Making Compliance
non_functional_requirements.description: The system shall comply with the Data (Use and Access) Act 2025 risk-based framework for automated decision-making once ICO guidance is finalised, with credit decisions likely classified as high-risk.

citation.source_reference: DUAA 2025 introduces a risk-based framework distinguishing low and high-risk ADM — credit decisions are likely high-risk

non_functional_requirements.reasoning: Emerging regulation. ICO guidance still pending but must be designed for.

non_functional_requirements.title: Consumer Understanding of Offers
non_functional_requirements.description: Pre-approved offer screens shall be designed so that a customer with no financial background can understand the total cost from the offer screen without external help. Validated via user testing with target comprehension rate >= 85%.

citation.source_reference: Enable and support retail customers to pursue their financial objectives
citation.source_location: Section 8.1 FCA Consumer Duty (Consumer understanding outcome)

non_functional_requirements.reasoning: Consumer Duty outcome requirement. Domain KB confirms acceptance criteria pattern.
